title: markhamia obtusifolia
intro: Markhamia obtusifolia, also known as the African Tree of Heaven or African Belleric Myrobalan, is a tree species native to tropical Africa. Traditionally, the plant's roots and bark have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, due to its medicinal properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, providing several health benefits such as immune system support and protection against oxidative stress. Markhamia obtusifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, and is also used in traditional medicine in various forms, including powders and capsules.
